revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hisagozuka Kofun (Himeji)]][[ja:瓢塚古墳 (姫路市)]][[zh:瓢塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Himeji, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hisagozuka Kofun | native_name =瓢塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Hisagozuka Kofun (Himeji), zenkei-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hisagozuka Kofun, posterior circular portion to the left | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Hisagozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.817789|N|134.599933|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|4.04|N|134|35|59.76|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Hisagozuka Kofun'''|瓢塚古墳||}}, also known as the {{nihongo|'''Yoro-Hisagozuka Kofun'''|丁瓢塚古墳||}}, is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Katsuhara neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139087|title=瓢塚古墳|trans-title=Hisagozuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Hisagozuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Ibo River|en|Ibo River|qid=Q11498396|lt=Ibo River|1=Ibo River}} Plain at an elevation of five meters. The tumulus is built on a flat land, but there is a hill on the east side, and it is highly possible that it was built by taking in a portion of the hill extension. It is presumed that the ruins of the nearby village were crushed during the construction because the embankment of the mound was found to contain a mixture of earthenware pieces. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is orientated to the south and has an overall length of around 100 meters, making it one of the largest in the Nishi-Harima region. The posterior circular portion of the mound is constructed in three tiers, but the anterior rectangular portion is not well-preserved, and its construction is uncertain. The scale of the mound is not clear because the mound has not been confirmed by [[archaeological excavation]], and lengths of anywhere from 98.8 to 109 meters appear in published literature. There are reports that ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found on the mound, but this is also uncertain and the location of any such artifacts is unknown. Likewise, it is presumed that large ''[[fukiishi]]'' were used on the mound, but these stones have also disappeared. No trace of a moat has been discovered. A pit-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was exposed at the southern edge of the rear circle, but from its location, it is presumed that a main burial chamber exists in the central part of the rear circle. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is presumed to have been built in the early Kofun period based on its shape. The scale of the mound is more than double that of the early Kofuns in the area, and is similar to the tombs of the kings of the Yamato Kingdom at that time.
The tumulus is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Aboshi Station|en|Aboshi Station|qid=Q3611088|lt=Aboshi Station|1=Aboshi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:San'yo Main Line|San'yo Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 109 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 58 meter diameter x 3-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 40 meters wide
==Gallery== <gallery> Hisagozuka Kofun (Himeji), zenkei-1.jpg|panoramic view with posterior circular portion to the left File:Hisagozuka Kofun (Himeji), funcho-1.jpg|posterior circular portion File:Hisagozuka Kofun (Himeji), kouenbu.jpg|anterior to poster File:Hisagozuka Kofun (Himeji), zenpoubu.jpg|posteior to anterior </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|qid=Q104629006|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{template-inline|Hisagozuka Kofun (Himeji)}} *[https://www.city.himeji.lg.jp/kanko/0000001884.html Himeji city home page] {{in lang|ja}}
